strategy_summary: The binary is a 32-bit ELF with partial RELRO, NX, and a stack canary. The main function reads 80 bytes into a buffer, then uses printf on the input, which suggests a format string vulnerability. We will use this to leak libc addresses from the GOT (printf, puts, read, system) and calculate the base address of libc. Then, we overwrite the 'x' variable in .bss to 4 using the format string vulnerability, which will trigger the call to system('/bin/sh').
